methods.description: Process for collecting and identifying cingulate specimens from various expeditions and museums for taxonomic study.

steps.description: The approximately 370 specimens of cingulates studied here were collected between 2013-2014 by the MLP-MACN-Duke University expeditions.
These specimens were identified through comparison with the type specimens and with more complete remains of specimens housed in the institutions mentioned below.
steps.searchable_summary: identify specimens by comparison with type specimens

methods.description: Procedure for measuring osteoderms using manual calipers and following specific descriptive terminology.

steps.description: Osteoderms were measured with manual calipers; the descriptive terminology follows Fernicola and Vizcaíno (2008), Krmpotic et al. (2009), Ciancio et al. (2013), and Francia and Ciancio (2013).

methods.description: Grouping of geographic references for localities reported for the SCF into eastern, central, and western areas.

steps.description: The geographic references for the localities reported for the SCF are grouped as follows: 1) eastern area, including the Atlantic coast, 2) central area, including RSC and Río Chalía (= Sehuen), 3) western area, including the Lago Argentino and Lago Posadas regions.

methods.description: Analysis of taxonomic richness of RSC cingulates across different levels and localities.

steps.description: The first considers the taxonomic richness referred to the RSC by Ameghino (1887, 1889) with that obtained based on the new remains.
steps.searchable_summary: compare historical and new taxonomic richness

steps.description: The second considers the richness among the three localities BB, SBB, and YH, based only on the new remains.
